title: Binary append optimization — in-place append when refcount==1
purpose: Binary construction via repeated append (`<<Acc/binary, NewByte>>`) is the most common binary-building pattern in Erlang/Gleam. Without optimization, each append allocates a new binary and copies all previous bytes — O(n^2) for building an n-byte binary. BEAM optimizes this: when a ProcBin has ref count 1 (only one reference exists) and has spare capacity, append writes directly into the existing allocation without copying. This turns O(n^2) into amortized O(n).
task: Implement the append optimization for ProcBin terms. When `bs_put_binary` or `iodata_append` detects a ProcBin target with ref count 1 and sufficient capacity, extend in-place instead of allocating a new binary.

requirements.title: SharedBinary with capacity tracking
requirements.spec: Extend SharedBinary to track allocated capacity separately from used length, enabling in-place growth.
acceptance: `SharedBinary::try_append(&mut self, bytes: &[u8]) -> bool` appends in-place if capacity allows, returns false if not
Capacity is only accessible when `Arc::strong_count == 1` (exclusive ownership)
Test: create SharedBinary with capacity 1024, append 100 bytes — succeeds without reallocation
Test: append beyond capacity returns false

requirements.title: BinaryBuilder detects appendable ProcBin
requirements.spec: When `bs_init` initializes a binary builder and the accumulator is a ProcBin with ref count 1, the builder reuses the existing SharedBinary instead of allocating a new one.
acceptance: `bs_init` checks if the source binary is a ProcBin with `Arc::strong_count == 1`
If appendable: builder wraps the existing SharedBinary, writes continue from the current position
If not appendable (ref count > 1, inline binary, or no ProcBin): existing behavior (new allocation)
Test: build binary in a loop — first iteration allocates, subsequent iterations append in-place

requirements.title: Capacity growth strategy
requirements.spec: When append exceeds capacity, grow the SharedBinary's allocation with a doubling strategy (matching BEAM's approach).
acceptance: When `try_append` fails due to capacity, allocate a new SharedBinary with `max(current_capacity * 2, current_len + new_bytes)`
Copy existing data to the new allocation
Update the ProcBin's pointer to the new SharedBinary
The old allocation is freed (Arc ref count was 1)
Test: build a binary byte-by-byte to 10,000 bytes — fewer than 20 allocations total (doubling strategy)

requirements.title: iodata_append optimization
requirements.spec: Apply the same optimization to the `iodata_append` BIF — when appending to a ProcBin with ref count 1, extend in-place.
acceptance: `iodata_append(ProcBin_ref1, data)` appends in-place when capacity allows
Test: repeated iodata_append to a sole-owner ProcBin is O(n), not O(n^2)

boundaries: SHALL NOT implement in-place append for inline binaries — only ProcBin with capacity
SHALL NOT modify binaries with ref count > 1 — copy-on-write semantics must be preserved
SHALL NOT break the immutability guarantee for shared binaries — only the sole owner can append
verification: cargo test -p beamr passes with all tests green
